agents.summarizationModelsHint: Models tried first for this agent's memory consolidation and context summarization, ahead of the global memory models and the agent's own model. Use to give an agent specialised models when the defaults refuse its content.
agents.shareCommonHint: Allow this agent to read/write the shared common directory and copy files to/from it.

chat.welcome: How can I help you today?
chat.welcomeDesc: Ask me about weather, settings, or any other tasks. I'm here to assist you.
chat.historyOpenFailed: Failed to open this chat history

empty.noConfiguredModelDescription: You need to configure at least one AI model with an API key before you can start chatting.
empty.noSelectedModelDescription: You have configured models, but none is set as default. Select a model before starting chat.

credentials.description: Manage OAuth and token-based credentials for supported providers.

openai.description: Supports browser OAuth, device code, and token login.

anthropic.description: Uses token login for Claude access.

antigravity.description: Uses browser OAuth for Google Cloud Code Assist.

logoutDialog.title: Logout provider?
logoutDialog.description: This will remove your saved credential for {{provider}}.

errors.popupBlocked: Unable to open a new tab. Please allow popups and try again.

device.title: OpenAI Device Login
device.description: Open the verification page and enter the code below. This page will refresh automatically.

models.description: Configure API keys for AI providers. Only configured models are available for chat.
models.noDefaultHintPrefix: No default model set yet. Click

defaultOnSave.label: Default Model
defaultOnSave.description: Automatically set this model as default after saving.

add.title: Add Custom Model
add.description: Add an OpenAI-compatible or native model endpoint.
add.modelNameHint: A short name used to identify this model in conversations.
add.modelIdHint: The raw model id the provider's endpoint expects (no protocol prefix). The provider supplies the wire protocol and base URL.
add.errorDuplicateModelName: Model alias already exists. Please use a different name.

delete.title: Delete Model?
delete.description: "{{name}}" will be permanently removed from your model list. This cannot be undone.

field.providerHint: The named provider this model is reached through. Manage providers on the Providers page.
field.authMethodHint: Authentication method: oauth, token. Leave blank for API key auth.
field.connectModeHint: Connection mode for CLI-based providers: stdio or grpc.
field.workspaceHint: Working directory for CLI-based providers (e.g. GitHub Copilot).
field.requestTimeoutHint: Maximum seconds to wait for a response. 0 = use default.
field.rpmHint: Maximum requests per minute. 0 = no limit.
field.thinkingLevelHint: Extended thinking budget: off, low, medium, high, xhigh, adaptive.
field.maxTokensHint: Maximum tokens the model may generate per response. Overrides the global default for this model only.
field.maxTokensFieldHint: Override the request field name for max tokens, e.g. max_completion_tokens.
field.reasoningEffortHint: Sets the OpenAI-style reasoning_effort request field on supported models (xAI Grok, OpenAI o-series). Accepted values: none, low, medium, high. "none" is sent explicitly (e.g. to disable reasoning); leave unset to omit the field entirely.
field.extraBodyHint: Free-form JSON merged into the OpenAI-compatible request body for per-provider knobs not modelled natively. Must be a JSON object. Reserved keys claimed by claw are rejected.
field.extraBodyInvalidJSON: Must be a valid JSON object.
field.extraBodyNotObject: Top-level value must be a JSON object.
field.extraBodyReservedKey: Key "{{key}}" is claimed by claw and cannot be overridden via extra_body.
field.dropParamsHint: Comma-separated request body fields to strip before sending (e.g. temperature). Use for parameters a model or upstream rejects — such as temperature on OpenRouter reasoning models that 404 under require_parameters. Applied after extra_body.
field.strictAlternationHint: For chat-only models that require alternating user/assistant turns and reject system/tool roles (e.g. Gemma): folds the system prompt into the first user turn, turns tool results into user turns, and merges consecutive same-role messages. Pair with Tools Disabled.

field.secretHintSet: A value is already set. Leave blank to keep it unchanged.

desc.token: Bot access token used to connect to the platform API.
desc.botToken: Bot token used to send and receive messages.
desc.appToken: App token used for Socket Mode connections.
desc.appId: Unique application ID used for authentication.
desc.appSecret: Application secret used for signing and authentication.
desc.encryptKey: Encryption key used to decrypt callback payloads.
desc.baseUrl: Platform API base URL. Official endpoint is used by default.
desc.proxy: HTTP proxy address for outbound network access.
desc.mentionOnly: Only respond when the bot is explicitly mentioned in group chats.
desc.typingEnabled: Display typing status while the assistant is generating a response.
desc.placeholderEnabled: Enable temporary placeholder messages before the final reply is sent.
desc.coalesceEnabled: Combine messages a sender sends in quick succession (e.g. a long paste the Telegram app splits) into one before the assistant responds.
desc.groupTriggerMentionOnly: In group chats, respond only when the bot is mentioned.
desc.groupTriggerPrefixes: Custom group-chat trigger prefixes, separated by commas.
desc.allowFrom: Allowed user or group IDs, separated by commas.
desc.allowOrigins: Allowed origin domains, separated by commas.
desc.clientId: Client ID used for platform authentication.
desc.welcomeMessage: Welcome message content for new sessions.
desc.allowTokenQuery: Allow token in URL query parameters.

agent.load_error: Failed to load agent support information.

skills.description: Skills are loaded from the workspace, global ClawEh home, and builtin directories.
skills.delete_description: "{{name}}" will be removed from workspace skills.
skills.viewer_description: Read the current effective SKILL.md content here.

tools.description: This view reflects whether each agent tool is enabled, disabled, or blocked by a missing prerequisite.

reasons.requires_linux: This tool only works on Linux hosts with the required device files exposed.
reasons.requires_skills: Enable `tools.skills` before this skill-registry tool can be used.
reasons.requires_subagent: Enable `tools.subagent` before the spawn tool can delegate work.
reasons.requires_mcp_discovery: Enable `tools.mcp.discovery` before MCP discovery tools become available.

config.load_error: Failed to load configuration. Please refresh and try again.
config.base_dir_hint: Base directory for all agent files. Each agent lives in <base>/<id> (the default agent in <base>/default). Leave blank for ~/.claw/agents; point it at another volume to relocate every agent.
config.common_dir_hint: Shared directory agents can read/write (default: <agents>/common). Set an absolute path to place it anywhere.
config.restrict_workspace_hint: Only allow file operations inside workspace.
config.allow_remote_hint: When enabled, shell commands can also run for remote sessions or non-local contexts. When disabled, shell execution stays limited to local safe contexts.
config.stream_tool_activity_hint: When enabled, the assistant's inter-tool narration and each tool's intermediate output are sent to the chat as it works. When disabled (default), the user receives only the final answer.
config.max_tokens_hint: Upper token limit per model response.
config.max_tool_iterations_hint: Maximum tool-call loops in a single task.
config.request_timeout_hint: Global default seconds to wait for a model response. Per-model timeouts override this (CLI models set it higher). 0 = built-in 120s default.
config.default_agent_hint: Handles messages that don't match any binding.
config.summarization_models_hint: Models tried in order for memory consolidation and context summarization across all agents. Each agent's own model is always used as a final fallback. Leave empty to use each agent's own model.
config.summarization_models_empty: No memory models configured — each agent uses its own model.
config.summarization_debug_capture_hint: Write the verbatim request and response of every summarization call to compact.jsonl in the agent's workspace. Debugging only — leave off in normal use.
config.compress_normal_percent_hint: Context % that triggers routine summarization. 0 = use default (50%).
config.compress_safety_percent_hint: Context % that triggers forced summarization when the normal threshold is missed. 0 = use default (80%).
config.compress_min_percent_hint: Context % floor — compression never fires below this level. 0 = use default (20%).
config.compress_message_threshold_hint: Trigger compression after this many new messages, regardless of context %. 0 = use default (20).
config.compress_retain_token_percent_hint: % of context window kept as recent uncompressed messages. 0 = use default (20%).
config.compress_retain_min_messages_hint: Minimum messages always kept in the uncompressed tail, regardless of token budget. 0 = use default (2).
config.archive_message_count_hint: Keep at most this many recent messages (also the agent's retrieval window). 0 = unlimited; falls back to the retention days below.
config.archive_days_hint: Permanently delete archived messages older than N days. 0 = no age limit (keep forever).
config.summary_max_count_hint: Keep at most this many recent context summaries. 0 = unlimited; falls back to the retention days below.
config.summary_retention_days_hint: Permanently delete context summaries older than N days. 0 = no age limit (keep forever).
config.session_mode_hint: Controls how the agent's memory is divided across users and platforms.
config.session_mode_unified_desc: One shared memory for the entire agent, across all users and channels.
config.session_mode_per_user_desc: Each person gets their own private memory.
config.session_mode_per_platform_desc: Each person has a separate memory per platform.
config.session_mode_per_account_desc: Like per-platform, but also separates by bot account.
config.monitor_usb_hint: Watch USB plug/unplug events when devices are enabled.
config.autostart_hint: Start ClawEh Web automatically when you log in.
config.autostart_unsupported: Launch at login is not supported on this platform.
config.server_port_hint: HTTP port used by ClawEh Web.
config.lan_access_hint: Allow access from other devices on your local network.
config.allowed_cidrs_hint: Only clients from these CIDR ranges can access the service. One per line or comma-separated. Leave empty to allow all.

sections.agent_defaults_desc: Default model, memory model chain, and the agent that handles unrouted messages — applied to agents without their own override.
sections.summarization_desc: Global memory model chain used for memory consolidation and context compression for every agent.
sections.context_management_desc: Controls when context is compressed, how much is retained, and how the archive is sized.

config.reset_confirm_desc: Are you sure you want to reset your unsaved changes back to the last saved state?
config.reset_success: Changes have been reset to the last saved state.
config.log_retention_days_hint: Days of rolled daily logs (YYYYMMDD-claw.log / -error.log) to keep. claw.log rolls at local midnight. 0 = keep forever.

mcp.load_error: Failed to load configuration. Please refresh and try again.
mcp.save_error: Failed to save MCP host configuration.
mcp.reset_success: Changes have been reset to the last saved state.
mcp.enable_desc: Controls whether claw runs an MCP server so CLI-based providers (claude-cli, codex-cli, gemini-cli) can call claw's host-side tools natively.
mcp.enabled_hint: When true, the MCP server always starts at daemon launch.
mcp.auto_enable_hint: When Enabled=false, still start the MCP server automatically if any enabled model uses a *-cli protocol. Explicit Enabled=true always wins.
mcp.transport_desc: Network address and endpoint path for the MCP Streamable HTTP listener.
mcp.listen_hint: host:port the MCP server binds to. Use 127.0.0.1 for local-only access.
mcp.endpoint_path_hint: HTTP path the MCP handler is mounted under. Must start with '/'.
mcp.tools_desc: Patterns determining which host-side tools are exposed to MCP clients. Fail-closed: an empty list exposes nothing.
mcp.tools_pattern_hint: One pattern per row. Supports '*' (all), 'prefix*' (prefix match, case-insensitive), or exact tool names.
mcp.tools_empty_warning: No patterns configured — no tools will be exposed.
mcp.client_hint: Upstream MCP servers claw connects out to (tools.mcp.servers). Their tools are exposed to direct API providers; CLI providers configure MCP separately. Edit as a JSON object keyed by server name. Leave empty for none.

memory.select_prompt: Select a memory store to view its domains and memories.
memory.load_error: Failed to load this memory store.
memory.no_memories: No active memories in this domain.

providers.description: Named providers define how models reach an upstream API or CLI. Each model references a provider by name.

field.name: Name
field.nameHint: A unique name used by models to reference this provider.
field.protocolHint: The wire protocol used to talk to this provider.
field.baseURLRequiredHint: Required for this protocol. e.g. https://api.example.com/v1
field.commandHint: Path or name of the CLI executable for this provider.
field.apiKeyPlaceholderSet: Leave blank to keep existing key
field.authMethodHint: Authentication method: oauth, token. Leave blank for API key auth.
field.strictCompatHint: Restrict requests to a strict subset of the OpenAI-compatible schema.
field.noParallelToolCallsHint: Send parallel_tool_calls=false so the model emits one tool call at a time.
field.responseFormatJSONHint: Request a JSON response_format from the provider.

add.title: Add Provider
add.description: Define a named provider that models can reference.
add.errorDuplicateName: A provider with this name already exists.

edit.title: Edit {{name}}
edit.apiKeyHint: A key is already set. Leave blank to keep it unchanged.

delete.title: Delete Provider?
delete.description: "{{name}}" will be permanently removed. This cannot be undone.
